mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, tight labor markets compress wage growth and reduce corporate margin pressure, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with accelerating housing starts indicate a synchronized consumption boom that drives corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample instances over a 4-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial output expansion, supply chain bottlenecks emerge and input costs spike, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of observations where INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5 percent month-over-month over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP above 2.5 percent signals robust aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue expansion and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not statistically higher than the baseline when GDPC1 quarterly growth exceeds 2.5 percent (t-test p > 0.05) across a 5-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations anchor higher, prompting the market to price in tighter monetary policy and depressing equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL inflation crosses 2.5 percent over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate reflect a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances over a 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, elevated mortgage rates suppress housing demand, which cascades into lower construction activity and reduced consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of observations where FEDFUNDS is above 1.5 percent and HOUST declines month-over-month over a 3-year rolling test.
